subject: [PATCH v2 022/110] cachefiles: use PRIino format for i_ino

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of PRIino format for i_ino in various cachefiles functions, specifically in debug messages and error handling. The author's response is neutral, explaining that the changes are necessary to prepare for the widening of i_ino via kino_t, but does not provide any indication that a fix will be made or that further revision is needed.

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch converts the i_ino format strings in the cachefiles code to use the PRIino format macro, which is a placeholder for the actual inode number type (32-bit or 64-bit). This change is made in preparation for the widening of the inode number type via the kino_t data structure. The changes affect several files in the fs/cachefiles directory and involve replacing printf-style format strings with PRIino macros to ensure that the correct inode number type is used regardless of whether it is 32-bit or 64-bit.
